scope.summary:
Goal: complete the 'melt-together' click-through by wiring the WP-011 context-menu surfaces and the shell NavigationTarget bus to the REAL WP-012 editor actions. This MT is dispatch-only glue: it does NOT build new context-menu infrastructure (the context-menu primitive already exists from WP-011 as context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs) and it does NOT build new editor actions (Rename Symbol, Quick Fix, Format Selection, Peek/Go-to-Def, Create-note-from-link already exist as MT-048/MT-049/MT-050/MT-008/MT-057). It connects the two: every context-menu entry must fire a real editor action handler, and every cross-surface click (quick-switcher, link click, node click) must route a NavigationTarget into the correct editor or pane addressed by stable id. The expected diff shape is: (a) EDIT the existing WP-011 module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/context_menu_surfaces.rs (~250-400 lines added) to define the two menu population functions (editor body menu, canvas/loom node menu) and bind each entry to the corresponding real action handler obtained from the pane registry (pane_registry.rs) and command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), (b) one CREATED module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/navigation_bus.rs (~200-350 lines) that completes the NavigationTarget enum, the dispatch function, and the per-pane registration table so a NavigationTarget resolves to a live pane by stable id and focuses/scrolls it, and (c) egui_kittest integration tests proving the menus fire real handlers and the bus routes targets to the right pane.

Files to CREATE or EDIT (EDIT context_menu_surfaces.rs in place since it already exists from WP-011, never duplicate WP-011 infra; CREATE navigation_bus.rs):
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/context_menu_surfaces.rs  -- EDIT the existing WP-011 module; this is the only place WP-012 context-menu wiring lives. Define `populate_editor_body_menu(ui: &mut egui::Ui, ctx: &AppContext, target: EditorMenuTarget)` and `populate_node_context_menu(ui: &mut egui::Ui, ctx: &AppContext, target: NodeMenuTarget)`. Each function adds entries by calling the WP-011 context-menu primitive (the existing context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs builder API, e.g. an entry(label, accesskit_id, on_activate) call); the `on_activate` closure must call the REAL action handler resolved from the command bus (command_registry.rs) / pane registry (pane_registry.rs) -- never a placeholder, never a TODO, never a no-op.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/navigation_bus.rs  -- CREATE this new module to complete the NavigationTarget bus. Define `enum NavigationTarget { EditorAtSymbol { pane_id: PaneId, symbol: SymbolRef }, EditorAtLocation { pane_id: PaneId, byte_offset: usize }, OpenNote { note_id: String }, RevealNode { pane_id: PaneId, node_id: String }, FocusPane { pane_id: PaneId } }` (match the variants the depended-on MTs actually emit; reconcile names against the live pane_registry.rs types). Define `fn dispatch(&self, target: NavigationTarget) -> Result<(), NavError>` that looks the pane up by stable id in pane_registry.rs, calls `registry.focus(pane_id)`, then calls the pane's `reveal(target)` method. Define a registration hook `fn register_nav_handler(pane_id: PaneId, handler: Arc<dyn NavHandler>)` so each editor pane registers how it reveals a target.

Files to EDIT (thin call-site changes only; do NOT reimplement these modules):
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs  -- add `pub mod navigation_bus;` (and ensure `pub mod context_menu_surfaces;` is declared) if not already declared; expose the singletons through the existing AppContext.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/quick_switcher.rs (the quick-switcher module from MT-030) -- replace its placeholder navigation call with `navigation_bus.dispatch(NavigationTarget::FocusPane{..})` / `EditorAtSymbol{..}` so selecting a result actually focuses the target editor/pane by stable id.
  - the code-editor pane module that owns the body context menu (the new WP-012 code_editor/ module created under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/) -- ensure its right-click handler calls `populate_editor_body_menu(...)` (it must already register a `NavHandler` via `register_nav_handler` so the bus can reveal a location/symbol inside it).
  - the canvas/loom node modules (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/canvas_board.rs and src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs) -- ensure right-click on a node calls `populate_node_context_menu(...)` and that node click emits the corresponding NavigationTarget (RevealNode / OpenNote).

Exact action bindings each context-menu entry MUST fire (no dead handlers on the required path):
  - 'Rename Symbol'    -> the real handler from MT-048 (rename-symbol command id, e.g. command_bus.dispatch_command("editor.rename_symbol", ctx)).
  - 'Quick Fix'        -> the real handler from MT-049 (quick-fix command).
  - 'Format Selection' -> the real handler from MT-050 (format-selection command).
  - 'Peek Definition' / 'Go to Definition' -> the real handler from MT-008 (peek / go-to-def code-nav, which is the only one of these that touches a backend API).
  - 'Create note from link' -> the real handler from MT-057 (create-note-from-link).
The canvas/loom node menu must offer the node-appropriate subset (Open note, Reveal node, Create note from link) bound to the same real handlers.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Locate the WP-011 context-menu primitive (context_menu.rs and the existing context_menu_surfaces.rs builder/entry API). Do not rebuild it. Use its builder/entry API verbatim. Each entry takes a label, a stable AccessKit id, and an activation closure.
  2. Locate the WP-011 pane registry (pane_registry.rs) and command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) on AppContext. The pane registry maps stable PaneId -> live pane handle; the command bus dispatches command-id -> handler. Resolve every menu action through one of these two -- never inline the action logic here.
  3. In context_menu_surfaces.rs, implement `populate_editor_body_menu`. For each of Rename Symbol, Quick Fix, Format Selection, Peek/Go-to-Def, Create-note-from-link, add an entry whose `on_activate` calls the real command via the command bus. Gate enablement on whether the action is available for the current selection (e.g. Rename Symbol disabled when no symbol under cursor) -- a disabled entry is acceptable, a dead-but-enabled entry is a FAIL.
  4. Implement `populate_node_context_menu` the same way for canvas/loom nodes (Open note via OpenNote NavigationTarget, Reveal node via RevealNode, Create note from link via MT-057 handler).
  5. In navigation_bus.rs, finalize the NavigationTarget enum to exactly cover what MT-008 (location/symbol), MT-030 (quick-switcher target), and MT-057/canvas (note/node) emit. Implement `dispatch`: resolve pane_id in pane_registry.rs; if absent return `Err(NavError::PaneNotFound)`; else focus the pane then call its registered NavHandler::reveal(target). For OpenNote, route through the note-open path that opens/focuses the note's pane then reveals it.
  6. Each editor/canvas pane registers a NavHandler in its constructor/mount via `register_nav_handler(pane_id, handler)` so the bus has a live reveal path. The handler is owned by the pane (this MT only ensures registration call sites exist and are wired; the reveal bodies belong to the panes' own MTs and are reused, not rewritten).
  7. Wire the MT-030 quick-switcher (quick_switcher.rs): on result confirm, build the NavigationTarget for the selected item (by its stable id) and call `navigation_bus.dispatch(target)`. Assert the correct pane is focused by stable id.
  8. AccessKit: every context-menu entry uses a stable AccessKit author_id from the existing WP-011 id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) (e.g. "ctxmenu-editor-rename-symbol", "ctxmenu-node-open-note"); reuse the registry, do not invent a parallel id scheme. The menu container and each item carry role=Menu / role=MenuItem so swarm agents can address and activate them.
  9. Keep this MT dispatch-only: NO new context-menu rendering infra, NO new editor-action logic, NO backend rewrite. The only backend touchpoint is indirect via MT-008's existing code-nav API; this module binds to that handler, it does not call the backend directly.
  10. Tests: an egui_kittest test that right-clicks the code body and asserts the menu shows Rename Symbol / Quick Fix / Format Selection / Peek entries and that activating each fires the real handler (assert the handler side-effect, e.g. command dispatched / pane state changed). An egui_kittest test that drives the MT-030 quick-switcher and asserts a NavigationTarget routes into the correct editor pane addressed by stable id. A cargo test asserting canvas/loom node context-menu actions dispatch to real handlers. A cargo test asserting NO context-menu entry on the required path hits a dead/placeholder handler (e.g. scan that every required entry's on_activate resolves to a registered command id or registered NavHandler).

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-008 (Peek/Go-to-Definition code-nav, backend-bound) must be merged.
  - MT-030 (quick-switcher) must be merged so there is a NavigationTarget producer to wire.
  - MT-048 (Rename Symbol), MT-049 (Quick Fix), MT-050 (Format Selection), MT-057 (Create-note-from-link) must be merged so the real handlers exist to bind.
  - WP-011 (cross-WP dependency) must provide the context-menu primitive (context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs), the pane registry (pane_registry.rs), the command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), and the AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) -- reused, not rebuilt.

Risk if missed: editor context menus and cross-surface navigation stay placeholder; right-click does nothing real and the quick-switcher cannot focus the right editor by id, leaving the 'melt-together' click-through incomplete and blocking MT-072.
acceptance_criteria: AC-070-1: Right-clicking the code editor body opens a context menu containing Rename Symbol, Quick Fix, Format Selection, and Peek/Go-to-Definition entries, and activating each fires the REAL editor action handler (MT-048/MT-049/MT-050/MT-008) — proven by egui_kittest asserting the handler side-effect, not a placeholder/no-op.
AC-070-2: The Create-note-from-link entry (present in editor body and/or node menu) fires the real MT-057 handler — proven by egui_kittest asserting a note-create dispatch.

AC-070-3: The NavigationTarget bus routes a quick-switcher (MT-030) selection into the correct editor/pane addressed by stable id — proven by egui_kittest asserting the focused pane id equals the target pane id.

AC-070-4: Canvas/loom node context-menu actions (Open note, Reveal node, Create note from link) dispatch to the real handlers — proven by cargo test asserting each entry resolves to a registered command id or registered NavHandler.

AC-070-5: No context-menu entry on the required path hits a dead/placeholder handler — proven by a cargo test that walks every required entry's on_activate and asserts it resolves to a live registered handler (FAIL if any required entry is a no-op/TODO).
AC-070-6: dispatch(NavigationTarget) returns Err(NavError::PaneNotFound) for an unknown pane id and Ok(()) after focusing+revealing for a known pane id — proven by unit test.

AC-070-7: No new context-menu infrastructure is introduced; this MT only calls the WP-011 context-menu primitive (context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs), pane registry (pane_registry.rs), command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), and AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) — proven by grep showing entries are added via the WP-011 builder and ids come from the existing registry.
AC-070-8: cargo test -p handshake-native passes with no failures across test_context_menu_surfaces and test_navigation_bus.

AC-070-9: AccessKit nodes present for the menu container (role=Menu) and each required item (role=MenuItem) using stable author_ids from the WP-011 id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) — proven by AccessKit tree dump in kittest.
proof_targets: PT-070-1: cargo test -p handshake-native context_menu_surfaces -- --nocapture  =>  all tests PASS, no panics
PT-070-2: cargo test -p handshake-native navigation_bus -- --nocapture  =>  all tests PASS, no panics
PT-070-3: egui_kittest harness: right-click code body, assert Rename/Quick Fix/Format/Peek entries present, activate each, assert real handler side-effect (command dispatched / state changed)
PT-070-4: egui_kittest harness: drive MT-030 quick-switcher, confirm a result, assert NavigationTarget routed into the correct pane (focused pane id == target pane id)
PT-070-5: cargo test: walk required context-menu entries and assert none resolves to a dead/placeholder handler
PT-070-6: AccessKit tree snapshot: assert menu container role=Menu and required MenuItems carry stable author_ids from the WP-011 id registry (accessibility/registry.rs)
implementation_notes: This MT is dispatch/glue only. Resolve every action through the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs: command-id -> handler) or pane registry (pane_registry.rs: PaneId -> live pane); never inline action logic and never duplicate WP-011 context-menu rendering.

Reuse the existing WP-011 context-menu builder/entry API from context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs verbatim; reuse the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) for every author_id (e.g. 'ctxmenu-editor-rename-symbol', 'ctxmenu-node-open-note').

NavigationTarget variants must exactly match what MT-008 (location/symbol), MT-030 (quick-switcher target), and MT-057/canvas (note/node) actually emit — reconcile names against the live pane_registry.rs types before finalizing the enum.

dispatch() flow: resolve pane_id in pane_registry.rs -> if absent return Err(NavError::PaneNotFound) -> else registry.focus(pane_id) then pane NavHandler::reveal(target). For OpenNote, route through the note-open path that opens/focuses the note pane then reveals.

Gate context-menu entry enablement on action availability for the current selection (e.g. Rename Symbol disabled when no symbol under cursor). A disabled entry is acceptable; a dead-but-enabled entry is a FAIL.

Each editor/canvas pane must register a NavHandler via register_nav_handler(pane_id, handler) at mount; this MT only ensures those registration call sites are wired — the reveal bodies are owned and reused from the panes' own MTs.

Only MT-008 (Peek/Go-to-Def) touches a backend API, and only indirectly via its existing handler; this MT performs NO direct backend calls and NO backend rewrite — any missing backend capability is a typed blocker, not a rewrite.

Cross-WP dependency (prose, not a depends_on id): WP-011 must provide the context-menu primitive (context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs), pane registry (pane_registry.rs), command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), and AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs); these are reused from WP-011, never rebuilt here.

NAV BUS = REUSE the MT-030 ShellNavigator, DO NOT FORK (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25, VERIFIED): the MT-030 navigation bus ALREADY EXISTS as crate::quick_switcher::ShellNavigator (open_document/open_code_symbol/open_loom_block/open_work_packet/open_micro_task/open_wiki_page seams), and MT-079 ALREADY wired open_document->mounted rich pane + open_code_symbol->mounted code pane (proven by document_hit_opens_mounted_rich_editor_tab). So navigation_bus.rs MUST be a THIN NavigationTarget layer that DELEGATES to the existing ShellNavigator open_* seams - NOT a parallel bus (RISK-070-4/MC-070-4 forking the substrate). The NavigationTarget enum MUST RECONCILE/map to the ShellNavigator existing open_* methods (RISK-070-2/MC-070-2 - reconcile against the live quick_switcher::ShellNavigator types, do NOT invent drifting variants). dispatch() resolves the pane via pane_registry, focuses, calls the pane NavHandler reveal; unknown/stale pane_id -> Err(NavError::PaneNotFound), NEVER panic (RISK-070-3/MC-070-3). AC-070-3 (quick-switcher routes to pane by id) is LARGELY DONE by MT-079 - verify + reconcile, do NOT re-wire quick_switcher.rs unless genuinely needed (it is NOT in allowed_paths; if a thin edit is unavoidable, disclose as a GLOBAL-SCOPE-005 deviation).

CONTEXT MENUS bind REAL handlers (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - VERIFIED the handlers exist): EDIT the existing WP-011 context_menu_surfaces.rs (it EXISTS; context_menu.rs is the WP-011 primitive - reuse its builder/entry API verbatim, NO new menu infra, RISK-070-4). populate_editor_body_menu binds: Rename Symbol -> MT-048 handler; Quick Fix -> MT-049 (code_editor/code_actions.rs - CODE_EDITOR_CTX_QUICK_FIX_AUTHOR_ID + the apply path); Format Selection -> MT-050; Peek/Go-to-Definition -> MT-008 (the only backend-touching one, indirect via its existing handler); Create-note-from-link -> MT-057. populate_node_context_menu (canvas_board.rs/graph node menus) binds the node subset (Open note via OpenNote NavigationTarget, Reveal node via RevealNode, Create-note via MT-057). EVERY entry on_activate calls the REAL handler resolved from the command bus / pane registry - NEVER a placeholder/no-op/TODO (RISK-070-1/MC-070-1, AC-070-5 - a cargo test walks every required entry + asserts it resolves to a registered command id or NavHandler; a dead-but-enabled entry is a FAIL). GATE enablement on availability (Rename disabled when no symbol under cursor - a disabled entry is OK, a dead-enabled entry FAILS). on_activate closures must NOT unwrap/expect on fallible results -> logged warning/NavError, never a frame-thread panic (RISK-070-6/MC-070-5).

ACCESSKIT + HYGIENE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): every context-menu entry uses a STABLE AccessKit author_id from the WP-011 id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) - reuse the code_editor/code_actions.rs ids where they exist (CODE_EDITOR_CTX_QUICK_FIX_AUTHOR_ID etc.) + the ctxmenu-editor-{action}/ctxmenu-node-{action} scheme; NO parallel id scheme (RISK-070-5/MC-070-5). menu container role=Menu + items role=MenuItem (AC-070-9). NO direct backend call (dispatch-only; only MT-008 touches backend indirectly via its handler); any missing real handler = typed BLOCKER not a placeholder (MC-070-6). NO Color32 literal (theme); external screenshot; full suite -j 2 (serialize); clippy clean. allowed_paths: context_menu_surfaces.rs (EDIT) + navigation_bus.rs (CREATE, delegates to ShellNavigator) + lib.rs (pub mod navigation_bus) + tests.
ports_from_react: WP-011 context-menu surfaces (context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs) — the editor body context menu and canvas/loom node context menu shapes (entry labels, grouping, AccessKit ids) ported to bind WP-012 real actions instead of placeholders
binds_backend_api: NONE (dispatch only) — this MT binds existing in-process editor-action handlers and the pane registry/command bus; the only backend touch is indirect via the already-bound MT-008 code-nav handler. No new or direct backend API calls.

risks: RISK-070-1: A context-menu entry is wired to a placeholder/no-op or a command id that is not actually registered, so the menu looks complete but right-click does nothing real on the required path.
RISK-070-2: NavigationTarget variant names drift from what MT-008/MT-030/MT-057 actually emit, so dispatch silently routes to the wrong pane or no pane.
RISK-070-3: A NavigationTarget references a stale/closed pane_id (e.g. quick-switcher result for a pane that was closed), causing a panic or a focus on the wrong pane.

RISK-070-4: This MT rebuilds context-menu infrastructure or editor-action logic instead of reusing WP-011 context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs primitives and the existing handlers, violating the dispatch-only scope and forking the 'melt-together' substrate.
RISK-070-5: AccessKit author_id collisions or a parallel id scheme are introduced instead of reusing the WP-011 id registry (accessibility/registry.rs), breaking swarm addressability of menu items.
RISK-070-6: A panic propagates out of an on_activate closure during an egui frame (e.g. handler unwrap), freezing the UI for all panes.
minimum_controls: MC-070-1: A cargo test walks every required context-menu entry and asserts its on_activate resolves to a registered command id or registered NavHandler; FAIL if any required entry is a no-op/TODO (enforces AC-070-5).

MC-070-2: Reconcile the NavigationTarget enum against the live emitter types from MT-008/MT-030/MT-057 and add a unit test that round-trips each emitted target through dispatch into the expected pane id.
MC-070-3: dispatch() must guard pane lookup: unknown pane_id returns Err(NavError::PaneNotFound) (never panic); add a unit test for the stale/closed pane_id path.

MC-070-4: Grep/test that context-menu entries are added via the WP-011 context_menu.rs / context_menu_surfaces.rs builder and that every author_id comes from the existing WP-011 id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) — no new menu-rendering infra, no parallel id scheme (enforces AC-070-7).
MC-070-5: on_activate closures must not unwrap/expect on fallible handler results; surface errors as a logged warning or NavError, never a frame-thread panic.
MC-070-6: Any missing real handler or backend capability must be raised as a typed blocker (BLOCKER object) rather than satisfied with a placeholder; the required path may not ship with a dead entry.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + VERIFIED quick_switcher::ShellNavigator (MT-030 nav bus, wired by MT-079) + context_menu_surfaces.rs/context_menu.rs exist (WP-011) + code_editor/code_actions.rs (MT-048/049/050 handlers + AccessKit ids)
corrections: nav bus ALREADY = quick_switcher::ShellNavigator (MT-030, MT-079-wired) -> navigation_bus.rs DELEGATES (thin NavigationTarget layer over open_* seams), does NOT fork; reconcile enum vs live ShellNavigator types

EDIT existing WP-011 context_menu_surfaces.rs (+ context_menu.rs primitive builder) - bind Rename(MT-048)/QuickFix(MT-049 code_actions.rs)/Format(MT-050)/Peek(MT-008)/Create-note(MT-057) REAL handlers via command bus; no dead entries
dispatch pane-not-found guard (no panic); enablement-gated entries; on_activate no unwrap/panic; reuse WP-011 AccessKit ids (code_actions.rs) - no parallel scheme
AC-070-3 quick-switcher->pane largely done by MT-079 (verify, do not re-wire); no backend call/Color32; external screenshot; -j 2

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_c000c249 (impl + 3-lens panel + harden)
commits: cba193ea feat MT-070 wire editor context menus + NavigationTarget bus
implementation_result.proofs: AC-070-1..9 + PT-070-1..6 PASS (AC-070-9/PT-070-6 PASS_WITH_DEVIATION); 5 nav-unit + 3 nav-integration + 24 context-menu (11 new) + 33 lib + regression (code_actions 7, rename 11, formatting 14, context_menu 13, quick_switcher 4) green; clippy clean. Dispatch-only MT (no screenshot).
implementation_result.steers_held: navigation_bus.rs DELEGATES to the MT-030 quick_switcher::ShellNavigator (NOT forked; quick_switcher.rs untouched - AC-070-3 already satisfied by MT-079, verified+reconciled). context_menu_surfaces.rs EDITED (WP-011 ContextMenu builder reused, no new infra). Context menus bind REAL handlers: Rename(MT-048)/QuickFix(MT-049)/Format(MT-050)/Peek(MT-008)/Create-note(MT-057) - captured as real EditorBodyMenuAction. TYPE-ENFORCED no-dead-handler (action enums have NO placeholder variant; disabled-not-dead-enabled). dispatch pane-not-found -> Err(NavError::PaneNotFound), never panics. AccessKit ids REUSE the existing code_actions.rs consts (CODE_EDITOR_CTX_RENAME_SYMBOL/QUICK_FIX/FORMAT_SELECTION/GOTODEF) - no parallel scheme.
implementation_result.deviations_honest: (1) AC-070-9: the menu-popup CONTAINER is a foreground Area/GenericContainer not Role::Menu (the WP-011 context_menu.rs primitive emits addressability on the ITEMS not the container; forcing Role::Menu would FORK the out-of-scope WP-011 primitive) - the items ARE Role::MenuItem (swarm-addressable, intent met). (2) the contract assumed command_bus.dispatch_command(editor.rename_symbol) + register_nav_handler/registry.focus/pane.reveal, but the LIVE baseline is a static AppCommand catalog + the ShellNavigator - the agent RECONCILED to the real types (EditorBodyMenuAction enum + ShellNavigator dispatch), binding to the REAL API not the assumed one.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: context_menu_surfaces.rs (EDIT) + navigation_bus.rs (new, delegates to ShellNavigator) + lib.rs + tests in allowed_paths.
